title: 10 possible side effects of Paeonia × suffruticosa (tree peony)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Paeonia × suffruticosa causes allergic reactions in some individuals. This is due to the presence of specific proteins and allergenic compounds in its pollen and sap, which can trigger an immune response in sensitive people. As a result, they may experience symptoms such as itchy skin, hives, and respiratory issues upon exposure to the plant or its products.

Paeonia × suffruticosa induces digestive problems in some individuals due to its unique chemical composition. The plant contains compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which can alter the gut microbiome and disrupt normal digestion. This may lead to symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and abdominal discomfort. Additionally, Paeonia × suffruticosa's ability to stimulate the production of certain enzymes in the digestive system can further contribute to these unwanted effects.

Paeonia × suffruticosa triggers dizziness attacks in some individuals due to its ability to interact with certain neurotransmitters in the brain. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, can bind to these neurotransmitters, disrupting normal functioning and leading to feelings of lightheadedness or vertigo. This effect is more pronounced when Paeonia × suffruticosa is used in large doses or for extended periods of time, allowing the bioactive compounds to accumulate in the system and amplify their impact on brain chemistry.

Paeonia × suffruticosa produces skin rashes. The allergenic properties of the plant's sap and the presence of coumarins, a class of organic compounds, may trigger an immune response in some individuals, leading to the development of skin rashes. Additionally, the plant's fragrant oils can cause irritation and allergic reactions, further contributing to the occurrence of skin rashes.

Paeonia × suffruticosa causes stomach pain due to its saponin and tannin content. These compounds can irritate the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, leading to discomfort, nausea, and stomach cramps. The anthraquinones present in Paeonia × suffruticosa may also contribute to this effect by altering gut motility and increasing water absorption in the intestines, exacerbating symptoms of indigestion and abdominal pain.

Paeonia × suffruticosa increases blood pressure due to its vasoconstrictive properties, which cause blood vessels to narrow. This can lead to a rise in systolic and diastolic blood pressure, potentially posing a risk to individuals with pre-existing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The increased blood pressure may be attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have a direct impact on vascular smooth muscle tone and vasoreactivity.

Paeonia × suffruticosa elicits anxiety symptoms due to its unique chemical composition. The plant contains alkaloids and glycosides that can interact with the body's serotonin levels, leading to feelings of unease and apprehension. Additionally, the plant's sedative properties may cause individuals to experience increased sensitivity to stimuli, further exacerbating anxiety symptoms.

Paeonia × suffruticosa causes insomnia issues due to its potential impact on the central nervous system. The plant's active compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, may interfere with normal sleep patterns by altering the body's natural sleep-wake cycle. Additionally, the stimulation caused by Paeonia × suffruticosa may increase alertness and make it difficult for individuals to fall asleep, leading to insomnia.

Paeonia × suffruticosa develops nausea complaints due to its high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides. These compounds can cause gastrointestinal irritation, leading to stomach upset and nausea in some individuals. The roots and rhizomes of Paeonia × suffruticosa contain these bioactive compounds, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. However, excessive consumption or sensitization can trigger adverse reactions, resulting in unpleasant gastrointestinal symptoms.

Paeonia × suffruticosa exhibits respiratory distress in some individuals. This side effect is believed to be caused by the plant's high levels of latex, which can release allergenic compounds into the air when its leaves or stems are bruised or damaged. These compounds can trigger an allergic reaction, leading to symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath in susceptible people.

intro: Paeonia × suffruticosa has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions. The causes of these side effects are often related to individual sensitivities or interactions with other medications. For example, consuming tree peony tea in excess can lead to nausea and vomiting, potentially disrupting daily routines and work schedules. Furthermore, an allergic reaction to the herb's proteins can cause skin rashes and swelling, affecting one's ability to participate in social activities and daily tasks.
intro_desc: Paeonia × suffruticosa has some side effects when used improperly, such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and diarrhea. These side effects are often caused by taking high doses or using the herb for extended periods without proper breaks. In severe cases, these side effects can worsen lives by leading to anaphylaxis, chronic digestive issues, or even organ damage. This can significantly impact daily activities, relationships, and overall well-being.
